reasoning_content:
The user wants the layout of the provided document extracted into a JSON object with bounding boxes.

1.  **Identify the main sections:**
    *   Header (Logo, Title, Document Info)
    *   Section 6.5 Electrical Characteristics (Table)
    *   Section 6.6 Timing Requirements (Table)
    *   Footer (Copyright, Links, Page Number)

2.  **Process Header:**
    *   Logo: Top left.
    *   Title: "TPS562200, TPS563200" Top right.
    *   Document ID: "SLVSCB0E..." Top right.
    *   Website: "www.ti.com" Top left.

3.  **Process Section 6.5:**
    *   Title: "6.5 Electrical Characteristics"
    *   Condition: "TJ = -40°C to 150°C..."
    *   Table: This is a complex table. I need to break it down row by row, or at least capture the main structure.
        *   Headers: PARAMETER, TEST CONDITIONS, MIN, TYP, MAX, UNIT.
        *   Sub-headers: SUPPLY CURRENT, LOGIC THRESHOLD, VFB VOLTAGE..., MOSFET, CURRENT LIMIT, THERMAL SHUTDOWN, OUTPUT UNDERTVOLTAGE..., UVLO.
        *   Data rows: I need to capture the text content and approximate bounding boxes for each cell.

4.  **Process Section 6.6:**
    *   Title: "6.6 Timing Requirements"
    *   Table: Similar structure to the previous one.
        *   Headers: MIN, TYP, MAX, UNIT.
        *   Sub-headers: ON-TIME TIMER CONTROL, SOFT START.
        *   Data rows.

5.  **Process Footer:**
    *   Copyright text.
    *   Feedback link.
    *   Page number.
    *   Product Folder Links.

6.  **Refine Bounding Boxes:** I will estimate coordinates based on a standard 1000x1000 grid or similar relative scale, but since I need to output specific coordinates, I will use the visual position. *Self-correction: The prompt asks for bboxes. I will generate them based on the visual layout.*

    *   *Header:*
        *   Logo: [36, 36, 70, 125]
        *   Title: [56, 746, 68, 910]
        *   Doc ID: [68, 646, 80, 910]
        *   URL: [68, 89, 125, 160]

    *   *Section 6.5:*
        *   Title: [94, 94, 110, 335]
        *   Condition: [116, 94, 128, 560]
        *   Table Headers: [132, 94, 148, 906]
        *   Rows... (This will be a long list).

    *   *Section 6.6:*
        *   Title: [763, 94, 779, 305]
        *   Table...

    *   *Footer:*
        *   Copyright: [950, 94, 962, 340]
        *   Feedback: [950, 705, 962, 870]
        *   Page: [950, 900, 962, 910]
        *   Links: [975, 365, 987, 635]

    *   *Table Content Strategy:* I will group the table content logically. The table is dense. I will extract the text and its box.

    *   *Let's start drafting the JSON structure.*

    *   *Header:*
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [36, 36, 70, 125], "text_content": "TEXAS INSTRUMENTS"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [68, 89, 125, 160], "text_content": "www.ti.com"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [56, 746, 68, 910], "text_content": "TPS562200, TPS563200"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [68, 646, 80, 910], "text_content": "SLVSCB0E – JANUARY 2014 – REVISED MAY 2023"}`

    *   *Section 6.5:*
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [94, 94, 110, 335], "text_content": "6.5 Electrical Characteristics"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [116, 94, 128, 560], "text_content": "TJ = -40°C to 150°C, VIN = 12V (unless otherwise noted)"}`
        *   Table Header Row: `{"bbox_2d": [132, 94, 148, 906], "text_content": "PARAMETER TEST CONDITIONS MIN TYP MAX UNIT"}`
        *   Row 1 (Supply Current): `{"bbox_2d": [152, 94, 164, 906], "text_content": "SUPPLY CURRENT"}`
        *   Row 2 (I(VIN)): `{"bbox_2d": [168, 94, 196, 906], "text_content": "I(VIN) Operating – non-switching supply current VIN current, TA = 25°C, EN = 5V, VFB = 0.8 V TPS562200 230 330 µA TPS563200 190 290"}` -> This is getting complex. I should break it down more granularly if possible, or just capture the row text. The prompt asks for "layout", so capturing the text within the bounding box is key.

    *   *Let's refine the table extraction.* I will iterate through the visible text blocks.

    *   *Table Row 1:* `{"bbox_2d": [152, 94, 164, 906], "text_content": "SUPPLY CURRENT"}`
    *   *Table Row 2:* `{"bbox_2d": [168, 94, 196, 906], "text_content": "I(VIN) Operating – non-switching supply current VIN current, TA = 25°C, EN = 5V, VFB = 0.8 V TPS562200 230 330 µA TPS563200 190 290"}`
    *   *Table Row 3:* `{"bbox_2d": [196, 94, 210, 906], "text_content": "I(VINSDN) Shutdown supply current VIN current, TA = 25°C, EN = 0 V 3 10 µA"}`
    *   *Table Row 4:* `{"bbox_2d": [214, 94, 226, 906], "text_content": "LOGIC THRESHOLD"}`
    *   *Table Row 5:* `{"bbox_2d": [230, 94, 252, 906], "text_content": "VEN(H) EN high-level input voltage EN 1.6 V"}`
    *   *Table Row 6:* `{"bbox_2d": [252, 94, 270, 906], "text_content": "VEN(L) EN low-level input voltage EN 0.6 V"}`
    *   *Table Row 7:* `{"bbox_2d": [270, 94, 292, 906], "text_content": "REN EN pin resistance to GND VEN = 12 V 225 450 900 kΩ"}`
    *   *Table Row 8:* `{"bbox_2d": [292, 94, 304, 906], "text_content": "VFB VOLTAGE AND DISCHARGE RESISTANCE"}`
    *   *Table Row 9:* `{"bbox_2d": [308, 94, 336, 906], "text_content": "VFB(TH) VFB threshold voltage TA = 25°C, VO = 1.05 V, IO = 10 mA, Eco-mode operation 772 mV"}`
    *   *Table Row 10:* `{"bbox_2d": [336, 94, 354, 906], "text_content": "TA = 25°C, VO = 1.05 V, continuous mode operation 758 765 772 mV"}`
    *   *Table Row 11:* `{"bbox_2d": [354, 94, 372, 906], "text_content": "I(VFB) VFB input current VFB = 0.8V, TA = 25°C 0 ±0.1 µA"}`
    *   *Table Row 12:* `{"bbox_2d": [372, 94, 384, 906], "text_content": "MOSFET"}`
    *   *Table Row 13:* `{"bbox_2d": [388, 94, 422, 906], "text_content": "RDS(on)h High side switch resistance TA = 25°C, VBST – SW = 5.5 V TPS562200 122 mΩ TPS563200 68 mΩ"}`
    *   *Table Row 14:* `{"bbox_2d": [422, 94, 456, 906], "text_content": "RDS(on)l Low side switch resistance TA = 25°C TPS562200 72 mΩ TPS563200 39 mΩ"}`
    *   *Table Row 15:* `{"bbox_2d": [460, 94, 472, 906], "text_content": "CURRENT LIMIT"}`
    *   *Table Row 16:* `{"bbox_2d": [476, 94, 510, 906], "text_content": "Iocl Current limit (1) DC current, VOUT = 1.05 V, LOUT = 2.2 µF TPS562200 2.5 3.2 4.3 A DC current, VOUT = 1.05 V, LOUT = 1.5 µF TPS563200 3.5 4.2 5.3 A"}`
    *   *Table Row 17:* `{"bbox_2d": [514, 94, 526, 906], "text_content": "THERMAL SHUTDOWN"}`
    *   *Table Row 18:* `{"bbox_2d": [530, 94, 564, 906], "text_content": "TSDN Thermal shutdown threshold(1) Shutdown temperature 155 °C Hysteresis 35"}`
    *   *Table Row 19:* `{"bbox_2d": [568, 94, 580, 906], "text_content": "OUTPUT UNDERTVOLTAGE AND OVERVOLTAGE PROTECTION"}`
    *   *Table Row 20:* `{"bbox_2d": [584, 94, 606, 906], "text_content": "VOVP Output OVP threshold OVP Detect 125% x Vfbth"}`
    *   *Table Row 21:* `{"bbox_2d": [606, 94, 630, 906], "text_content": "VUVP Output Hiccup threshold Hiccup detect 65% x Vfbth"}`
    *   *Table Row 22:* `{"bbox_2d": [630, 94, 652, 906], "text_content": "tHiccupOn Hiccup On Time Relative to soft-start time 1 ms"}`
    *   *Table Row 23:* `{"bbox_2d": [652, 94, 674, 906], "text_content": "tHiccupOff Hiccup Off Time Relative to soft-start time 7 ms"}`
    *   *Table Row 24:* `{"bbox_2d": [678, 94, 690, 906], "text_content": "UVLO"}`
    *   *Table Row 25:* `{"bbox_2d": [694, 94, 728, 906], "text_content": "UVLO UVLO threshold Wake up VIN voltage 3.45 3.75 4.05 V Hysteresis VIN voltage 0.13 0.32 0.55"}`
    *   *Footnote:* `{"bbox_2d": [738, 94, 750, 250], "text_content": "(1) Not production tested"}`

    *   *Section 6.6:*
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [763, 94, 779, 305], "text_content": "6.6 Timing Requirements"}`
        *   Table Header: `{"bbox_2d": [783, 94, 799, 906], "text_content": "MIN TYP MAX UNIT"}`
        *   Row 1: `{"bbox_2d": [803, 94, 815, 906], "text_content": "ON-TIME TIMER CONTROL"}`
        *   Row 2: `{"bbox_2d": [819, 94, 837, 906], "text_content": "tON On time VIN = 12 V, VO = 1.05 V 150 ns"}`
        *   Row 3: `{"bbox_2d": [837, 94, 855, 906], "text_content": "tOFF(MIN) Minimum off time TA = 25°C, VFB = 0.5 V 260 310 ns"}`
        *   Row 4: `{"bbox_2d": [859, 94, 871, 906], "text_content": "SOFT START"}`
        *   Row 5: `{"bbox_2d": [875, 94, 893, 906], "text_content": "tss Soft-start time Internal soft-start time, TA = 25°C 0.7 1 1.3 ms"}`

    *   *Footer:*
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [950, 94, 962, 340], "text_content": "Copyright © 2023 Texas Instruments Incorporated"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [950, 705, 962, 870], "text_content": "Submit Document Feedback"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [950, 900, 962, 910], "text_content": "7"}`
        *   `{"bbox_2d": [975, 365, 987, 635], "text_content": "Product Folder Links: TPS562200 TPS563200"}`

    *   *Review and Format:* Ensure all coordinates are reasonable and text is accurate. The table structure is the hardest part. I will combine cells that are visually merged or closely related in the same row to keep the JSON manageable but accurate to the layout.

    *   *Correction on Row 16 (Current Limit):* The text spans multiple lines. I'll capture it as a single block.
    *   *Correction on Row 18 (Thermal Shutdown):* Similar multi-line structure.
    *   *Correction on Row 25 (UVLO):* Multi-line structure.

    *   *Final check of coordinates:*
        *   Header y: ~36-80
        *   Section 6.5 start: ~94
        *   Section 6.6 start: ~763
        *   Footer start: ~950
        *   X range: 94 to 906 (table width)

    *   *Constructing the JSON.*
